title: Project Management Software Pricing Guide for 2026
description: A transparent breakdown of project management software pricing across free, team, and enterprise tiers to help you budget accurately.
body:
Project management software pricing varies significantly across platforms and tiers. Understanding what each tier offers and where limitations begin is essential for choosing a platform that fits both your team's needs and your budget. This guide provides a clear comparison of pricing tiers across the leading project management platforms.

Pricing for SaaS tools evolves frequently. The figures in this guide were verified as of July 2026, but checking each vendor's current pricing page before making a purchasing decision is recommended.

## Jira Pricing

Jira by Atlassian offers a free tier supporting up to 10 users with core Agile features including Kanban and Scrum boards, backlog management, and roadmaps. The Standard plan is priced at $7.91 per user per month and adds project-level permissions and support. The Premium plan at $14.54 per user per month includes advanced roadmaps, sandbox environments, and cross-project planning. Enterprise pricing is custom and includes dedicated support, 99.99% SLA, and unlimited automation.

## Monday.com Pricing

Monday.com offers a free tier with basic boards, column types, and templates, supporting up to 2 seats. The Basic plan is $9 per seat per month and adds timeline and Gantt views. The Standard plan at $12 per seat per month is the most popular tier, including integrations and automation. The Pro plan at $19 per seat per month adds private boards and time tracking. Enterprise pricing is custom with advanced security and compliance features.

## Asana Pricing

Asana offers a free tier with core task management and collaboration features. The Premium plan adds timeline, goals, and advanced search. The Business plan includes portfolio management, goals, and workload features. Enterprise plans add SAML SSO, data export, and priority support. Current pricing for each tier is detailed on Asana's pricing page, which reflects the most up-to-date figures.

## ClickUp Pricing

ClickUp offers a free tier with a broad set of views and features. The Unlimited plan adds unlimited storage and dashboards. The Business plan includes advanced automation and timelines. Enterprise plans include dedicated infrastructure and priority support. ClickUp's current pricing is published on its pricing page.

## Trello Pricing

Trello offers a free tier with core board and card features. The Standard plan adds unlimited boards, advanced checklists, and custom fields. The Premium plan includes timelines, dashboards, and table views. Enterprise pricing includes advanced admin and security controls. Current pricing is available on Trello's pricing page.

## Choosing Based on Budget

For small teams with limited budgets, the free tiers of Jira or Monday.com provide solid foundations for software development and general project management respectively. Teams that need more features should evaluate ClickUp's free tier for its extensive view options or consider Asana's Premium plan when they outgrow the free tier. Enterprise organizations with compliance requirements should budget for the enterprise tiers of their chosen platform, which typically include SSO, audit logs, and dedicated support.
author: PilotStack Team
